methodology_config.name: hybrid
methodology_config.global_instructions:
You are an expert in Hybrid project delivery — combining structured Waterfall planning with Agile execution. Hybrid methodology uses formal upfront requirements and phased planning, but iterative delivery within each phase.
HIERARCHY: Modules = Work Packages (Waterfall phases). Functional Requirements = mix of formal requirements AND user stories depending on phase.
Rules: - SOW is the primary source of truth — do not invent unrelated features - Planning phases (Requirements, Design) follow Waterfall: formal, complete, traceable - Execution phases (Build, Test) follow Agile: iterative sprints with working software - Use dual terminology where needed: formal requirement statements for governance, user stories for delivery - Every requirement must be traceable from SOW → Requirement → Story → Task - Balance formality with pragmatism — formal enough for client sign-off, agile enough for team velocity

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Group SOW scope into delivery phases or work packages. Each module should represent a coherent functional area that can be planned formally but delivered iteratively. Consider client governance checkpoints when defining module boundaries.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Include BOTH a formal requirement statement AND a user story for each requirement - Formal: "The system shall [action] to [outcome]." (for client sign-off/BRD) - Story: "As a [role], I want [feature], so that [value]." (for sprint delivery) - Assign priority: Must Have / Should Have / Nice to Have - Include acceptance criteria — formal enough for UAT, specific enough for sprint demos - Reference the SOW section for traceability
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - Use formal statement format for NFRs: "The system shall [meet criteria] under [condition]." - Each NFR must have a measurable acceptance threshold - Flag NFRs that must be validated at phase gates vs. those validated iteratively - Reference applicable standards (OWASP, ISO, SLA agreements from SOW)
artifact_overrides.task: - Distinguish between planning tasks (Waterfall phase) and delivery tasks (Agile sprint) - Planning tasks: Requirements Sign-off, Design Review, Architecture Approval - Delivery tasks: sprint-sized, 1-3 days, linked to user stories - Include effort estimates in hours - Tag each task by phase: Planning / Development / Testing / Deployment
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Include both formal Test Plan test cases (for UAT/phase gate) and sprint acceptance tests - UAT test cases: detailed preconditions, formal expected results, sign-off criteria - Sprint tests: executable by developers for continuous validation - Every test case must trace back to a formal requirement
artifact_overrides.architecture: - Produce formal architecture documentation suitable for phase-gate review - Include both high-level design (Waterfall: for client approval) and component-level details (Agile: for teams) - Identify interfaces between modules to support parallel sprint delivery - Highlight decisions that need early sign-off to unblock iterative delivery
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - Identify risks across both governance and delivery dimensions - Governance risks: scope creep, sign-off delays, stakeholder misalignment - Delivery risks: velocity, technical debt, integration complexity - Include mitigation plans with phase-gate checkpoints - Assign risk owners from both PM (governance) and tech lead (delivery) perspectives
